title: Update documentation
description: Update root CLAUDE.md to mention v2 isolation. Update rewrite-gallformers-v2 to mark hosting question resolved. Tasks 11.1-11.2.

title: Finalize define-v2-foundation open items
description:
## Context

The `define-v2-foundation` proposal has been reviewed and updated. Four open items remain that require research and decisions before the proposal can be applied.

## Open Items

### 1. Backup Strategy (tasks.md 2.1)

Research and decide on a backup strategy for the v2 Fly.io SQLite database:

**Options to evaluate:**
- **Litestream**: Streams SQLite changes to S3 automatically. Low complexity, proven pattern.
- **Manual backups**: Scheduled `fly ssh sftp get` to backup location
- **Fly.io native**: Check if Fly.io has built-in volume backup options
- **Other**: Any other viable approaches

**Decision criteria:**
- Reliability (can we restore from disaster?)
- Complexity (how much setup/maintenance?)
- Cost (S3 storage, etc.)
- Recovery time (how fast can we restore?)

### 2. Environment Variables and Secrets Strategy (tasks.md 2.2)

Define and document the complete environment variable strategy:

**Required env vars to identify:**
- `DATABASE_PATH` (already defined)
- `PROD_HOST` (for download-db)
- Auth0 credentials (CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET, etc.)
- S3 credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, etc.)
- Any others needed

**Document for each:**
- Local dev: How set? (.env file pattern)
- CI/CD: How set? (GitHub secrets)
- Fly.io: How set? (fly secrets)

**Deliverable:** Update design.md with a new "Environment Variables" section listing all vars and how to configure them in each environment.

### 3. CI/CD Strategy (tasks.md 2.3)

Define the CI/CD pipeline for v2 code:

**Questions to answer:**
- Should v2 CI be in a separate workflow file or added to existing CI.yml?
- What checks run on PRs touching `v2/`? (Go tests, Svelte build, type checking)
- What triggers deployment? (push to main with v2 changes only?)
- Should deployment wait for tests to pass first?

**Deliverable:** Draft the actual workflow YAML (or key structure) in design.md.

### 4. Rollback Strategy (tasks.md 2.4)

Document the rollback procedure:

**Questions to answer:**
- How do we detect a bad deployment? (monitoring, manual check, health endpoint)
- What are the exact rollback commands? (`fly releases`, `fly deploy --image <previous>`)
- What about database changes? (SQLite has no point-in-time recovery without backups)
- Who can perform rollback? (anyone with Fly.io access)

**Deliverable:** Add a "Rollback Procedure" section to design.md with step-by-step instructions.

## Agent Instructions

Work through these items one at a time with the user:

1. Present the options/considerations for each item
2. Ask clarifying questions if needed
3. Get the user's decision
4. Update the relevant files (design.md, tasks.md) with the decisions
5. Mark items complete as you go

After all items are resolved, the proposal should be ready for final review and application.

## Files to Update

- `openspec/changes/define-v2-foundation/design.md` - Add new sections for decisions
- `openspec/changes/define-v2-foundation/tasks.md` - Mark items complete as resolved

title: Create daily snapshot workflow
description: Create .github/workflows/db-snapshot.yml with scheduled daily run. Add AWS credentials to GitHub secrets. Update Makefile download-db to point to public S3 URL. Tasks 13.5-13.6.

title: Allow dashes in genus name when editing gall species name
description: In the gall admin page, when editing a species name, the validation does not allow dashes '-' in the genus portion of the name. Dashes should be valid characters in genus names (e.g., for hyphenated genera). Need to update the species name validation logic to permit dashes.

title: Add genus name uniqueness validation endpoint
description: Create or extend API to check if a genus name already exists. Similar to how family name checking works via nameExistsEndpoint. May be able to use existing generaSearch or need a dedicated exact-match endpoint.

title: Document rollback strategy
description: Document rollback procedure: how to identify bad deployment, step-by-step rollback commands, database considerations. Task 2.4.

title: Review and confirm v2 decisions
description: Review design.md analysis and confirm: v2/ subdirectory structure, Fly.io hosting with single-app architecture, Makefile-coordinated local dev, trunk-based git workflow. Tasks 1.1-1.5.

title: Document status page procedures
description: Document status page usage in runbooks: how to manually add incidents, acknowledge/resolve. Update v2/runbooks/incident-response.md with status page procedures. Tasks 12.3-12.4.

title: Test and verify backup system
description: Deploy with Litestream enabled. Verify data appears in S3. Test restore procedure. Update restore-database runbook with specific commands. Task 13.7.

title: Verify local development
description: Test make dev from v2/ starts both servers. Test curl localhost:8080/health returns 200. Test curl localhost:5173 returns 200. Test make download-db copies database. Tasks 8.1-8.4.

title: Define V2 Technical Foundation
description: Epic tracking all work for the define-v2-foundation OpenSpec proposal. Sets up v2 directory structure, Fly.io hosting, Go API scaffold, Svelte web scaffold, CI/CD, and deployment verification.

title: Scaffold Svelte Web
description: Create v2/web/ with SvelteKit: npm create svelte, configure static adapter, placeholder index page 'Gallformers v2 - Coming Soon', Makefile. Verify npm run dev/build work. Tasks 7.1-7.5.

title: Remove unnecessary [REPLACE] bracket escaping in SQL
description:
In libs/db/taxonomy.ts:632, the SQL uses [REPLACE] with square bracket escaping:

```sql
SET name = [REPLACE](name, SUBSTRING(...), ...)
```

The brackets are SQL Server-style identifier escaping. While SQLite accepts this syntax, it's unnecessary since REPLACE is not a reserved word.

**Fix:** Simply change `[REPLACE]` to `REPLACE`.

This is cosmetic/cleanup only - the code works correctly as-is.

title: Define environment variables strategy
description: Define env vars strategy: required vars for v2 (DATABASE_PATH, PROD_HOST, etc.), local dev setup (.env file pattern, .env.example template), Fly.io secrets (fly secrets set). Task 2.2.

title: Cleanup existing Fly.io attempt
description: Destroy existing gallformers Fly.io app if present, remove fly.toml from repo root, remove Dockerfile.fly from repo root. Tasks 3.1-3.3.

title: Create v2 directory structure
description: Create v2/ directory with: CLAUDE.md (agent isolation rules), Makefile (oaks pattern), fly.toml, Dockerfile, .env.example, .gitignore. Tasks 5.1-5.7.

title: Add Rename custom action to genus EditableDataTable
description:
In pages/admin/taxonomy.tsx, add a 'Rename' entry to the customActions prop of the EditableDataTable for genera. Should:
- Only enable when exactly one genus is selected
- Open the RenameGenus modal
- Handle the rename callback to update local state

title: Fix incorrect Litestream claims in CLAUDE.md and project.md
close_reason: Fixed incorrect Litestream claims in CLAUDE.md, project.md, and copilot-instructions.md

title: Test genus rename end-to-end
description:
Test the full rename flow:
1. Select genus → click Rename → enter new name → verify validation
2. Submit change → verify genus name updated in UI
3. Check species names updated correctly in database
4. Verify no broken references or side effects
5. Test edge cases: duplicate names, special characters, long names

title: Create image processing proposal (follow oaks pattern)
close_reason: Created add-image-processing proposal following oaks pattern

title: Configure Fly.io Litestream secrets
description: Add LITESTREAM_ACCESS_KEY_ID and LITESTREAM_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY to Fly.io secrets. Task 13.4.

title: Create proposal for reference article rendering in v2
description: Address markdown pipeline, glossary auto-linking, frontmatter handling for ref/ articles in Svelte.

title: Rename genus modal incorrectly rejects reverting to original name before save
description: Steps to reproduce: 1) Select a genus (e.g., Foo) and open Rename modal. 2) Rename it to FooX and close modal. 3) Reopen Rename modal and try to rename FooX back to Foo. 4) Modal rejects with 'name already exists' error. The issue is that nameExistsCallback checks the database, but the original name (Foo) still exists there since the user hasn't clicked Submit yet. The modal should either track the original name and allow reverting, or the validation should account for unsaved local state.

title: Disable or add validation to inline genus name editing in taxonomy admin
description: The EditableDataTable allows inline editing of genus names in the Genera table on admin/taxonomy. This bypasses all validation that the Rename modal provides (duplicate name check, empty check). When the form is submitted, the inline edit saves to the database via updateExistingGenera() and also updates all species names via updateExistingSpecies(). Either disable inline editing for the name column, or add validation equivalent to the Rename modal.

title: Scaffold Go API
description: Create v2/api/ structure: cmd/server/main.go with /health endpoint, internal/handlers/, go.mod, Makefile. Verify go build works, make run starts server, curl localhost:8080/health returns 200. Tasks 6.1-6.6.

title: V2 Proposal Review Follow-ups
description:
Epic containing follow-up tasks from the comprehensive review of the rewrite-gallformers-v2 proposal conducted in January 2026.

## Agent Prompt

Use this prompt to work through these tasks with the user:

---

I'm helping you work through the follow-up tasks from the V2 proposal review. Here's what needs to be done:

**P0 - Critical:**
1. `gallformers-7ln`: Fix incorrect Litestream claims in CLAUDE.md and project.md
   - The docs claim Litestream is in use for backups, but it's not
   - Update both files to reflect reality (no automated backups currently)
   - Reference define-v2-foundation for the decision on v2 backup strategy

**P2 - Before Implementation:**
2. `gallformers-9ns`: Create image processing proposal following oaks pattern
   - Analyze how oaks handles image processing
   - Create a proposal for how v2 will handle image upload/resize/processing

3. `gallformers-v88`: Define Go API + Svelte deployment architecture
   - Decide: Single app (Go embeds static files) vs two apps vs CDN+API split
   - Document in define-v2-foundation or add-go-api proposal
   - Consider CORS, Dockerfile structure, cost implications

4. `gallformers-buj`: Create proposal for reference article rendering
   - Address markdown pipeline for ref/ articles in Svelte
   - Handle glossary term auto-linking
   - Handle frontmatter processing

**P3 - During Implementation:**
5. `gallformers-iyy`: Address CORS configuration in add-go-api proposal
   - Note that Go API needs CORS headers for Svelte frontend
   - Document approach in add-go-api proposal

Which task would you like to start with?

---

## Context

The proposal edits from the review session have already been applied to:
- openspec/changes/rewrite-gallformers-v2/proposal.md
- openspec/changes/rewrite-gallformers-v2/design.md
- openspec/changes/rewrite-gallformers-v2/tasks.md
- openspec/changes/rewrite-gallformers-v2/specs/platform/spec.md

Key changes made:
- Removed all SSG references (v2 will be full SPA)
- Removed audit trail/undo scope (future features, not v2)
- Removed geographic expansion and maps (future features)
- Relaxed phase dependencies to allow overlap
- Added "Deferred to Sub-Proposals" section
- Fixed technical audit path reference

title: Research backup strategy
description: Research backup strategy options: Litestream vs manual 'fly ssh sftp get' vs other approaches for SQLite on Fly.io. Document recommendation. Task 2.1.

title: Configure and verify CI/CD
description: Add FLY_API_TOKEN to GitHub secrets. Test CI runs on PR with v2 changes. Test deploy pipeline on push to main. Tasks 9.3-9.5.

title: Review and verify existing species name update logic
description:
Analyze updateExistingSpecies() in libs/db/taxonomy.ts (lines 628-642) to verify the raw SQL correctly handles edge cases.

**Current Implementation:**
```sql
UPDATE species
SET name = [REPLACE](name, SUBSTRING(name, 1, INSTR(name, ' ') - 1), '${g.name}') 
WHERE id IN (
    SELECT st.species_id
    FROM taxonomy AS t
    INNER JOIN speciestaxonomy AS st ON t.id = st.taxonomy_id
    WHERE t.id = ${g.id} 
);
```

**Edge Cases to Verify:**
- Species with unusual naming patterns (not 'Genus epithet' format)
- Species names that already don't match their genus
- Empty or null cases
- Species with multiple words after genus (subspecies, etc.)

**Security Concern:**
- SQL injection risk: genus name is string-interpolated directly into SQL
- Consider using parameterized queries

**Test approach:**
- Test the REPLACE logic manually with sample data
- Check existing species in DB for non-standard naming patterns

title: Address CORS configuration in add-go-api proposal
description: Go API needs CORS headers for Svelte frontend during dev (different ports) and potentially prod depending on deployment architecture.
close_reason: Not needed initially - single app architecture means no CORS. Will address during CDN migration if/when that happens.

title: Create RenameGenus modal component
description:
Create a modal component for renaming a genus. Options:
1. Reuse/adapt existing EditName component (used for Family rename)
2. Create a simpler dedicated component

Should:
- Accept current genus name as default
- Validate new name is not empty
- Check uniqueness before allowing save
- Only allow renaming one genus at a time

title: Create S3 bucket and IAM for backups
description: Create gallformers-backups S3 bucket in us-east-1, enable versioning, configure public read for public/ prefix. Create IAM user with S3 read/write access, generate access key. Tasks 13.1-13.2.

title: Create Fly.io app and volume
description: Create Fly.io app named 'gallformers', create Fly.io volume for database, configure initial secrets (fly secrets set DATABASE_PATH=...). Tasks 4.1-4.3.

title: Add Litestream to Docker image
description: Create v2/litestream.yml config. Update Dockerfile to install Litestream. Update CMD to use Litestream wrapper. Tasks 13.3.

title: Define v2 API error response format in foundation spec
description: Define a consistent error response format for all v2 API endpoints. Should cover: 400 validation errors, 401 unauthorized, 404 not found, 409 conflict, 500 internal errors. Include error code, message, and optional details. This should be documented in the define-v2-foundation design doc and implemented as a shared helper in the Go API.

title: Define CI/CD strategy for v2
description: Define CI/CD strategy: test pipeline (Go tests, Svelte build, type checking), PR checks for v2 code, deploy pipeline on push to main. Task 2.3.

title: Setup CI/CD workflows
description: Create .github/workflows/ci-v2.yml for tests/build on PRs. Create .github/workflows/deploy-v2.yml for deployment (trigger on push to main with v2/ changes). Tasks 9.1-9.2.

title: Add Genus Rename Feature to Admin Taxonomy Page
description:
Add a formal Rename option for Genera in the admin/taxonomy page. Currently only Family can be renamed (via a modal). When a genus row is selected in the Genera table, only Move and Delete actions are available. This epic adds a Rename button that opens a modal similar to Family rename.

**Context:**
- Inline editing of genus names exists but is easy to do accidentally and lacks validation
- Backend already handles genus rename propagation to species names (updateExistingSpecies in taxonomy.ts)
- Need to verify species name update handles edge cases correctly
- Should validate new genus name doesn't already exist

**Risk Areas:**
- Species name update uses raw SQL (REPLACE on first word)
- Transaction safety if rename fails partway
- Impact on search, URLs, cached data

title: Define Go API + Svelte deployment architecture
description: Clarify whether Go binary embeds static files, or separate apps, or CDN+API split. Affects Dockerfile, CORS, deployment complexity.
close_reason: Decided: Start with Go-embedded static files (single app). Migration to S3+CloudFront documented for future if edge performance needed.

title: Verify Fly.io deployment
description: Test fly deploy from v2/ directory. Verify curl https://gallformers.fly.dev/health returns 200. Verify placeholder page loads. Test CI/CD auto-deploys. Tasks 10.1-10.4.

title: Set up Upptime status page
description: Set up Upptime in separate repo or branch. Configure .upptimerc.yml for v2 endpoints, enable GitHub Pages, configure health check intervals. Tasks 12.1-12.2.

title: SQL injection risk in updateExistingSpecies - use parameterized queries
description:
In libs/db/taxonomy.ts:628-642, the updateExistingSpecies() function directly interpolates g.name and g.id into SQL strings without parameterization.

**Current code:**
```typescript
const sql = `UPDATE species
    SET name = [REPLACE](name, SUBSTRING(name, 1, INSTR(name, ' ') - 1), '${g.name}')
    WHERE id IN (... WHERE t.id = ${g.id} ...)`;
return db.$executeRaw(Prisma.sql([sql]));
```

**Risk:** Low - endpoint requires Auth0 authentication (admin-only), but violates defense-in-depth.

**Fix:** Use Prisma's tagged template literal for parameterized queries:
```typescript
return db.$executeRaw`UPDATE species SET name = ... WHERE ...`;
```

Note: The REPLACE logic itself is correct and handles edge cases properly.
